title: Rhythm: morning routine
description: Rhythm exercise with a flowing sentence - find your steady tempo

tips.text: Nothing new to learn here. We settle into a tempo and let the hands do honest, even work.
Sit square to the keyboard and keep your eyes off your fingers. Repeat each word at one calm pace, counting to yourself if it helps. The goal is regularity, not speed.

tips.title: Keep the beat
tips.text: Hold an even spacing between keystrokes, like a metronome you cannot see. Slow and regular beats fast and ragged every time. Even keystrokes read like clean copy.

tips.title: Rhythm: rise
tips.hint: Repeat at one even pace: rise rise rise. Hold the same beat from first key to last.

tips.title: Rhythm: morning
tips.hint: Repeat at one even pace: morning morning morning. Hold the same beat from first key to last.

tips.title: Rhythm: share
tips.hint: Repeat at one even pace: share share share. Hold the same beat from first key to last.

tips.text: Repetition at a steady pace is how a movement becomes a habit. Get the rhythm right and the speed arrives on its own, quietly, without being chased.
Worked through every step? Open the trainer below and make one full run on the lesson text. That run saves your progress and unlocks the next lesson.

character_tips.anna: Rhythm lesson - turn on the metronome and follow the beat.
character_tips.maxim: Smooth tempo beats sprint-and-stop. Find your groove.
